_rationale:
NOTHING is hidden from the scan -- every element, including chart internals, is evaluated. levelCapWhenNeedsReview caps axe results flagged needs-review (axe's `incomplete` bucket: 'axe could not determine this; a human must verify it') to WARNING instead of ERROR. Genuine axe `violations` are untouched and still fail as errors (see node_modules/pa11y/lib/runners/axe.js: only the incomplete set is passed needsFurtherReview=true). This surfaces -- rather than hides -- axe's uncertainty. Verified 2026-06-13: with the cap removed, axe returns ~56 color-contrast results, ALL of them incompletes (needsFurtherReview=true), 0 genuine violations. Most are chart-internal SVG / useHTML foreignObject labels (the pie category+percent legend tags + bar-chart axis labels) whose background axe's pixel sampler cannot resolve near the JS-rendered Highcharts SVG (bgOverlap, contrastRatio reported as 0). The only non-chart elements are real HTML that happens to sit directly beneath a tall JS-rendered chart -- the methodology

and its DOI link (under the inaction bar chart) and the 'What we learned'

(under the action donuts) -- MANUALLY computed at 9.64:1, 8.41:1 and 10.95:1, far above the 4.5:1 AA threshold (see docs/ACCESSIBILITY.md). The exact count shifts slightly with chart geometry; all are dark ink on light panels and verified passing. Charts are progressive enhancement: the accessible representation is the always-present data (visible with scripting off / in print; collapsed to screen-reader-only once the interactive chart is ready, so AT still reads it); a static graphic renders with scripting off. Note: chromeLaunchConfig (executablePath / --no-sandbox) is environment-specific and supplied at runtime, e.g. --config of a local file that merges this one.
